title: Auxiliary laps-to-pit multitask model
summary: Recent F1 pit-stop papers report gains from predicting both next-lap pit probability and distance-to-stop; the auxiliary horizon task can stabilize rare positive labels and sharpen borderline pit-window rows that plain binary AUC models treat similarly.
rationale: Recent F1 pit-stop papers report gains from predicting both next-lap pit probability and distance-to-stop; the auxiliary horizon task can stabilize rare positive labels and sharpen borderline pit-window rows that plain binary AUC models treat similarly.
implementation_hint: Keep the current feature pipeline, but add a second target such as capped laps-until-next-pit within each driver-race sequence. Train either a two-head NN or a pair of tabular models where the horizon prediction is fed back as an out-of-fold feature into the final binary classifier. Validate strictly by whole race groups.
expected_effect: Better discrimination around pit-window edges, especially medium-probability rows where current handcrafted features are already saturated.
risk: If the auxiliary target is noisy around final stints or missing future pits, it can add variance unless constructed causally and capped carefully.
